meaning: adj.活泼的,快活的,[植]多年生的
examples: He had three pretty, vivacious daughters.
Holding her hand was Bhanu, with a 5 vivacious smile and loads of baby fat.
At the age of twenty-one, in 1906, Modigliani arrived in Paris as a vivacious dandy.
And so has been the story of jazz, ever since its birth in the vivacious city of New Orleans.
